question: Are there any anomalies or outliers in the time series of methane emissions for the Waste sector in any country?

analysis.chart_type: Line chart with z-score anomaly overlay (detail by country)
analysis.design_rationale: A multi-faceted line chart, highlighting countries with outlier points, effectively illustrates the time series structure of methane emissions for the Waste sector and pinpoints where (country/year) and when (temporally) sudden emission anomalies occur. Outliers are revealed using z-scores computed per country, visualized as red markers atop the time series. This approach enables users to spot both sustained and single-year spikes/drops across 1990-2018 for each country, which would be missed by aggregation.
analysis.target_insight: Temporal anomalies or sudden shifts in Waste sector emissions for specific countries, and which countries/years contain significant outliers compared to their own historical trends.

chart_encodings.x: Year (from 1990 to 2018, columns reshaped to row variable)
chart_encodings.marker (highlight): Anomaly points (outlier detection by country over time, shown in red circles)
chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: All countries faint, but country with outlier points in more saturated color; anomaly points overlaid; subplot (facet) for up to 10 selected countries with most/strongest anomalies

transformations: Reshape DataFrame from wide (columns 1990-2018) to long, yielding columns 'Country', 'Year', 'Emission'
For each Country, compute the z-score over its time series, flagging emission values with |z| > 2 as anomalies
Select top 9 countries with most anomaly points (if ties, highest magnitude anomalies), for one subplot panel per country
data_preparation_steps: Compute, for each Country, the z-score for the 'Emission' time series
Flag |z| > 2 as anomalies; annotate anomaly mask
Count anomalies per country, select 9 with highest count (or anomaly score sum for rank ties)
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib as mpl
df = pd.read_csv('csv_c_squared\2.csv')
# Filter
waste_df = df[df['Sector']=='Waste'].copy()
years = [str(y) for y in range(1990,2019)]
waste_long = waste_df.melt(id_vars=['Country'], value_vars=years, var_name='Year', value_name='Emission')
waste_long = waste_long.dropna(subset=['Emission'])
waste_long['Year'] = waste_long['Year'].astype(int)
# Z-score per country
def zflag(group):
    vals = group['Emission']
    z = (vals - vals.mean()) / vals.std(ddof=0)
    group['z'] = z
    group['is_anomaly'] = np.abs(z) > 2
    return group
waste_long = waste_long.groupby('Country', group_keys=False).apply(zflag)
# Most anomalous countries
anomaly_counts = waste_long.groupby('Country')['is_anomaly'].sum().sort_values(ascending=False)
top_countries = anomaly_counts.head(9).index.tolist()
waste_top = waste_long[waste_long['Country'].isin(top_countries)]
# Facet lineplot
fig, axes = plt.subplots(3, 3, figsize=(16, 12), sharex=True, sharey=True)
axes = axes.flat
for idx, country in enumerate(top_countries):
    ax = axes[idx]
    sub = waste_long[waste_long['Country']==country]
    ax.plot(sub['Year'], sub['Emission'], color='gray', alpha=0.5, lw=1)
    # Highlight anomalies
    anomalies = sub[sub['is_anomaly']]
    ax.plot(sub['Year'], sub['Emission'], color='tab:blue', lw=2, zorder=1)
    ax.scatter(anomalies['Year'], anomalies['Emission'], s=60, color='red', edgecolor='black', zorder=2, label='Anomaly')
    ax.set_title(country, fontsize=12)
    ax.set_xlabel('Year')
    if idx % 3 == 0:
        ax.set_ylabel('Methane Emissions (MTCO2e)')
# Legends and layout
handles = [mpl.lines.Line2D([0], [0], color='tab:blue', lw=2, label='Emissions'),
           mpl.lines.Line2D([0], [0], marker='o', color='red', lw=0, label='Anomaly', markerfacecolor='red', markeredgecolor='black')]
fig.legend(handles=handles, loc='upper center', ncol=2, fontsize=13, frameon=False)
fig.suptitle('Methane Emissions (Waste Sector) \n Anomalies (Country-level z-score > 2) Highlighted, 1990-2018', fontsize=17)
fig.tight_layout(rect=[0, 0, 1, 0.94])
plt.show()
